title: Back-to-School Pandemic Toolbox for Parents and Caregivers
summary: Pediatric cases of COVID-19 are continuously rising in the midst of back to school re-openings. The Centers for Disease Control and Preventions updated Guidance for COVID-19 Prevention in K - 12 Schools to keep children safe through a layered prevention strategy is essential for schools to follow and consistently implement.
body: Pediatric cases of COVID-19 are continuously rising in the midst of back to school re-openings. The Centers for Disease Control and Preventions updated Guidance for COVID-19 Prevention in K - 12 Schools to keep children safe through a layered prevention strategy is essential for schools to follow and consistently implement. Beyond school-specific strategies, we share our back to school pandemic toolbox as parents of children under the age of twelve who are not yet eligible to be vaccinated with risk reduction measures that can be readily applied and implemented as children head back to school for in-person learning. Other respiratory infections are also on the rise like RSV and parainfluenza viruses so this toolbox can help mitigate and reduce the risk of other circulating respiratory viruses. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: With pediatric COVID-19 cases rising during school reopenings, the article emphasizes the critical need for schools to implement the CDC's updated layered prevention strategy for K-12 settings. It specifically addresses parents and caregivers of children under twelve, who are ineligible for vaccination, by providing a practical 'pandemic toolbox' of risk reduction measures for in-person learning. This toolbox is also framed as useful for mitigating other increasing respiratory infections like RSV and parainfluenza. The piece is presented as expert guidance from the Belfer Center to help families navigate the ongoing public health challenges of the school year.
result.main_content_zh: 随着学校重新开学，儿童COVID-19病例持续增加。文章强调，学校必须遵循并持续实施美国疾病控制与预防中心（CDC）更新的K-12学校COVID-19预防指南，该指南通过分层预防策略保障儿童安全。文章特别针对12岁以下尚未符合疫苗接种资格的儿童的父母和看护者，分享了一个“疫情工具箱”，其中包含可立即应用和实施的风险降低措施，以应对儿童返校进行面对面学习的情况。该工具箱也有助于减轻和降低其他正在传播的呼吸道病毒（如呼吸道合胞病毒和副流感病毒）的风险。本文是贝尔弗中心提供的专家指导，旨在帮助家庭应对学年中持续的公共卫生挑战。
